claim_or_expectation: BAA and DPA are presented as founder-signable procurement templates.
evidence: `public/baa.html` says the page is a working draft and that executed copies are sent on letterhead; no executable BAA/DPA artifact was found in the repo.
risk: Enterprise buyers can treat a marketing draft as an available legal artifact.
recommended_action: Keep the page framed as draft until an executable template file, owner, and version history exist.

claim_or_expectation: The BAA page says PHI is returned or destroyed within 30 days and certified in writing.
evidence: `/v1/account/delete` only marks the tenant `_deleted` and cancels billing best-effort; it does not purge concepts, compile jobs, observations, cache, artifacts, or emit a deletion certificate.
risk: Contract language promises a deletion workflow the product cannot prove today.
recommended_action: Build a tenant purge/certification workflow or narrow BAA deletion language to manual contractual handling.

claim_or_expectation: The BAA page lists Vercel, Railway, and Stripe with claims about PHI exposure and BAA coverage where PHI is touched.
evidence: Vercel config proxies API traffic to Railway; capture and compile routes can receive sensitive prompts/examples; no subprocessor contract evidence exists in the repo.
risk: Subprocessor claims can become inaccurate as deploy topology changes or if regulated data enters hosted routes.
recommended_action: Maintain a dated subprocessor register with data categories, regions, contracts, and deployment-specific PHI status.

claim_or_expectation: Privacy says job metadata is retained for 90 days, then aggregated and deleted.
evidence: `compile_jobs` are inserted and listed, but no retention job, TTL, purge command, or aggregation path was found.
risk: Privacy retention promises can be false in production and create deletion-request exposure.
recommended_action: Implement retention enforcement with tests or remove the 90-day deletion promise until operationalized.

claim_or_expectation: Privacy says account records are deleted within 30 days of account delete.
evidence: Account delete soft-deletes only the tenant record; earlier tenant-data audit confirmed private/public concepts and artifacts can remain.
risk: Privacy policy overpromises erasure and can conflict with GDPR/contract expectations.
recommended_action: Define delete versus deactivate, then implement cascade purge or change the policy text.

claim_or_expectation: Privacy references `kolm account export` and `kolm account delete`.
evidence: Root CLI search found `whoami`/account GET behavior, but no account export/delete/cancel command implementation.
risk: Users are directed to privacy controls that do not exist in the CLI.
recommended_action: Add CLI account commands or route users to implemented web/API flows only.

claim_or_expectation: Terms say compile data is used only for the requested compile job and not retained after completion.
evidence: Compile jobs retain task text and artifact metadata; artifacts can contain eval/test material; no post-job cleanup path was found.
risk: Terms can conflict with actual storage and artifact behavior.
recommended_action: Rewrite terms around exact retained fields and add cleanup controls for submitted examples/evals/artifacts.

claim_or_expectation: Audit-log page says every data-touching action leaves a per-tenant audit entry queryable in JSON/CSV.
evidence: `GET /v1/audit/log` always returns 503 beta response with empty entries; no durable audit table or JSON/CSV export route exists.
risk: Regulated buyers can expect an audit feature that is not shipped.
recommended_action: Gate the page behind beta language, or implement durable per-tenant audit logging before selling it.

claim_or_expectation: Audit-log page says tenants can opt in via `POST /v1/account/audit-log`.
evidence: No route for `/v1/account/audit-log` was found; router only documents the future opt-in in the 503 message.
risk: A user following the documented opt-in path cannot enable the feature.
recommended_action: Add the opt-in route with state, tests, and UI feedback or remove the instruction.

claim_or_expectation: Audit-log page says raw prompt/response text is deletable via `/v1/observations/purge`.
evidence: No `/v1/observations/purge` route exists; capture routes automatically persist prompt up to 8000 chars and response up to 16000 chars in `observations`.
risk: The page claims a deletion control while captures persist sensitive text without purge.
recommended_action: Implement observation purge/export/retention controls and make capture raw-text retention explicit.

claim_or_expectation: Capture comments say upstream provider keys are stripped and never persisted.
evidence: Code forwards upstream keys without storing them, but prompt/response content is stored automatically in observations.
risk: The key-handling claim is supported, but raw-content retention can surprise users.
recommended_action: Separate upstream-key non-retention from prompt/response retention in docs and add opt-out/redaction controls.

claim_or_expectation: Security page says Sigstore is live and Cosign signatures exist on every published artifact.
evidence: No local Cosign, Rekor, release signing, provenance, or release workflow artifacts were found.
risk: Supply-chain trust claims can fail procurement review immediately.
recommended_action: Either ship release signing artifacts/workflows or mark Sigstore/Rekor as roadmap/enterprise design.

claim_or_expectation: Security page says one CycloneDX SBOM exists per release.
evidence: No SBOM, CycloneDX, provenance, or release workflow files were found in the repo.
risk: Buyers asking for software composition evidence will find no downloadable artifact.
recommended_action: Add SBOM generation to release CI and link actual versioned SBOM files.

claim_or_expectation: Security page labels SLSA Level 3 as target, but also says release tags trigger a hermetic build with provenance.
evidence: No release-tag workflow or SLSA provenance generation was found.
risk: The target label is defensible, but adjacent prose reads like shipped build provenance.
recommended_action: Move the hermetic-build wording under roadmap until the workflow exists.

claim_or_expectation: security.txt exists and is served from `/.well-known/security.txt` and `/security.txt`.
evidence: `server.js` serves both paths and `public/.well-known/security.txt` has Contact, Encryption, Expires, Canonical, and Policy fields.
risk: The basic vulnerability contact mechanism exists.
recommended_action: Keep the file current, serve `text/plain`, and add automated checks for expiry and referenced resources.

claim_or_expectation: Security page and security.txt point researchers to a PGP key.
evidence: `public/.well-known/pgp-key.txt` is descriptive text with a placeholder-style block; `.asc` is referenced but not present; `gpg` is unavailable here to verify import.
risk: Researchers may be unable to encrypt sensitive reports despite the disclosure page asking them to do so.
recommended_action: Publish an actual armored public key, add `.asc`, and test import in CI.

claim_or_expectation: Security page promises paid bounty and a hall of fame at `/security/researchers`.
evidence: No `/security/researchers` static page or route was found; security.txt has no acknowledgments URL.
risk: Researchers may rely on bounty terms that are not actually published.
recommended_action: Publish bounty scope/reward terms and acknowledgments page, or remove the paid-bounty claim.

claim_or_expectation: Healthcare page says PHI stays on hosts customers control and artifacts run inside the BAA boundary.
evidence: The page also says customer-hosted bridge is an Enterprise design path; current hosted capture/compile routes can receive and store sensitive data.
risk: Healthcare buyers can miss the distinction between architecture target and current hosted behavior.
recommended_action: Add a prominent hosted-service limitation: no PHI in public hosted routes until a signed BAA and customer-hosted bridge are in place.

claim_or_expectation: Healthcare page names `.kolm` artifacts with K-scores and sizes.
evidence: Cookbook pages exist for some names, but no matching `.kolm` artifact files were found in the repo inventory.
risk: Claimed regulated artifacts can look fabricated if the binary and reproducible benchmark are missing.
recommended_action: Publish actual artifacts, manifests, receipts, and benchmark commands or label them as examples/templates.

claim_or_expectation: Legal page says no client document touches a third-party API at runtime.
evidence: This can be true for local artifact runs, but compile-time examples and capture flows still use hosted services unless customer-hosted deployment is contracted.
risk: Legal buyers may treat runtime no-egress as full lifecycle no-egress.
recommended_action: Separate runtime guarantee from compile-time data flow and customer-hosted deployment requirements.

claim_or_expectation: Pricing/serve/trust pages present optional Sigstore/Rekor anchoring as Enterprise capability.
evidence: Receipt code emits HMAC receipts and comments Ed25519/public-key receipts as roadmap; no Rekor/Sigstore implementation was found.
risk: Enterprise trust features can be sold before the anchoring path exists.
recommended_action: Gate external anchoring copy behind shipped code and tests, or mark as roadmap.

claim_or_expectation: `/v1/spec` response says receipts support EU AI Act/SOC 2 evidence.
evidence: Receipts are HMAC-based and useful evidence, but audit log, retention, public-key verification, and governance controls remain incomplete.
risk: Standards references can be read as compliance readiness rather than evidence support.
recommended_action: Phrase standards language as potential supporting evidence, not compliance coverage.

claim_or_expectation: Enterprise page promises a quarterly compliance binder PDF with receipt-chain, K-score, subprocessor, and incident reports.
evidence: No report generation, incident log, subprocessor delta, or PDF artifact workflow was found.
risk: Procurement can request a deliverable that cannot be produced reliably.
recommended_action: Create a compliance-report generator and sample binder or relabel as planned.

claim_or_expectation: DPA page promises controller/processor roles, subprocessor notice, subject-right assistance, deletion/return, and audits.
evidence: The public wording aligns with GDPR Article 28 categories, but product controls for export, erasure, audit logging, subprocessor registry, and retention are incomplete.
risk: Contract text may outpace the operational controls needed to satisfy processor obligations.
recommended_action: Build a DPA control checklist tied to implemented routes, logs, and manual procedures before signing regulated customers.

claim_or_expectation: Healthcare and BAA pages discuss PHI/health workflows but do not mention FTC Health Breach Notification Rule edge cases for non-HIPAA consumer health contexts.
evidence: Official FTC guidance says non-HIPAA health apps/PHR vendors and related entities can have breach notification obligations.
risk: A healthcare-adjacent deployment could fall outside HIPAA but still carry health breach obligations.
recommended_action: Add legal review checklist for HIPAA-covered versus non-HIPAA consumer health deployments.
source: public/healthcare.html; FTC Health Breach Notification Rule guidance
